notes: v310 — 🥇 VOL RECORD AND 🔷 BLOCK DAY NOW GRADED BOTH WAYS (a sharp beta-tester question: 'does Block day rate up-days and down-days separately like the other signals?' — it didn't, and it should). These two flags fire whether the price rose or fell that day, and heavy volume means opposite things in each direction: with the price UP it's buying pressure, with the price DOWN it's selling pressure — one mixed average hides both stories. So each now shows a split line under its row in the 📋 Signal report card: 🟢 PRICE-UP FIRES are judged like a bullish flag (how often higher five days later, average move, beat the market or not), and 🔴 PRICE-DOWN FIRES are judged like a warning — for a warning, LOWER afterwards means it WORKED, exactly how 📉 Dist? is graded. Flat days stay in the overall count but in neither sub-bucket, so nothing is forced into a story it doesn't fit. Don't be surprised to see one flag 'beat the market' and 'work as a warning' at the same time — that's the point: its green fires and red fires are different animals, now graded separately, the same idea as the Vol Surge chip's green-vs-red applied to the report card. The report-card lesson covers the new split. ALSO RECENT — THE REPORT CARD NOW GRADES COMBINATIONS: do two or three flags firing TOGETHER beat any flag alone? A new 🧩 Combinations section in the 📋 Signal report card answers exactly that. It takes every pair and triple of 🚀 Breakout, 🕵️ Informed?, 🏦 Inst?, ⤴ MA cross, 🥇 Vol record and 🔷 Block day, counts a combo ONLY when its flags fired on the same share on the same day, and grades it with the identical honest maths as the single flags — % higher five trading days later and average move, against the all-shares baseline. Every combo row also shows EACH FLAG'S SOLO AVERAGE right beside it, so 'is the pair better than its parts?' is answered at a glance, and a 'Strongest this period' headline sits on top. The honesty rules are built in: combos that fired fewer than 10 times are hidden (with a count of how many were hidden) because tiny samples are noise; anything under 30 fires carries a 'small sample' tag; 📉 Dist? is a bearish flag and is excluded from bullish combos by design; and quiet weeks show an honest 'nothing co-fired often enough to grade' box rather than made-up numbers. One important reading note, printed right in the section: combos fire far less often than single flags, so their results swing more week to week — a combo that tops the card once is a LEAD worth watching across the next several report cards, not proof. The report-card lesson in 🎓 Lessons covers the new section. ALSO RECENT — HISTORY OPEN/HIGH/LOW: NOW FILLS FOR PAST DAYS TOO (follow-up to v307). If your History window showed the new columns only on the Today row with '—' for earlier days, this one's for you. Two subtle causes: the app's daily data top-up saves a slim close-and-volume row for each new day — so the RECENT days on your screen had no stored opens/highs/lows even though older days did, which meant the automatic 'no data → fetch live' retry never triggered; and when it did trigger, a same-session memory cache handed the same slim rows straight back. Both fixed: the live retry now fires whenever the recent days on screen are missing their opens/highs/lows (not only when everything is missing), and a forced live fetch now truly goes to the API — then saves the full figures on your device, so each share is healed PERMANENTLY the first time you open its History while online. What you'll see: open a share's History, '…' for a moment, then Open, High and Low filled for every date. ALSO RECENT — HISTORY NOW SHOWS THE FULL DAY: OPEN, HIGH AND LOW FOR EVERY DATE. The 📊 History window used to show Open only on the Today row with '—' for every past day — and two new columns join it: HIGH and LOW, the day's true trading extremes. Why the dashes happened: the panel fetches each share's daily opens once (one API call, then remembered) — but it kept ONLY the opens, and if that first attempt came back empty (typically because the share's saved offline data predates opens being stored), the empty answer was cached forever — dashes for life. Fixed properly: the fetch now keeps open, high AND low for every day; if your saved data has no opens it automatically retries once straight from the live API; and a genuinely empty answer backs off for a minute instead of being remembered forever. You'll see '…' briefly while it fetches, then real figures — '—' now only ever means the data source truly has none for that day. Reading the new columns: the high and low frame the day's battle — a close near the HIGH means the day's push held into the finish (buyers won); a close well off the low means buyers stepped in during the day; a wide high-to-low range on heavy volume marks a genuinely contested session. On phones the Open/High/Low trio hides with the other secondary columns so Date · Close · Change · Volume still fit cleanly. As always: end-of-day figures, estimates for research — not advice. ALSO RECENT — THE WHOLE EXCHANGE LOADS BY DEFAULT (mystery of the missing 800 solved). A beta tester noticed one copy of the app loading 2,060 ASX stocks while another loaded 2,859. The cause: the 'What to load from the API (live)' type chips in Setup defaulted to SHARES ONLY on a fresh install — quietly skipping every ETF, LIC, REIT, option, preference share and bond (~800 securities), and because the choice is remembered per device, two devices could disagree forever without saying why. Two changes: 1) fresh installs now tick ALL types, so out of the box you get the COMPLETE exchange — matching the load preset that was already named 'All shares'; if you previously saved your own chip selection, nothing changes — your choice is kept exactly. 2) whenever the chips DO skip securities, the load status now says so in plain sight — e.g. '2,060 stocks · live · 799 skipped by type' — so a shortened list is always explained, never silent. Prefer the leaner shares-only view? Untick the other chips once in Setup ▸ What to load — it's remembered on that device, and the skipped-by-type note will confirm it's your setting doing it. ALSO RECENT — THREE DISPLAY FIXES (thanks to a sharp-eyed beta tester on a Windows laptop). 📡 LIVE MARKET RADAR: while the app is loading signals — especially the long 'using the data saved on this device' run — the radar tiles used to sit at 0 until the very last moment, which looked broken next to a table full of numbers. The tiles now count UP as each batch of shares finishes, so you can watch the market picture assemble in real time; when the status line shows '✓ Everything loaded', any tile still at 0 is genuinely zero. 📌 FROZEN TICKER COLUMN: on narrower windows (or with Windows display scaling), the results table is wider than the screen — and scrolling sideways to the Signals column used to scroll the ticker names right off the edge, leaving rows you couldn't identify. The Ticker/Name column (with its News/Chart/Buy links) now stays FROZEN on the left while the rest of the table slides underneath it — the same trick the portfolio tables already use on phones. 🧢 THE TOP BAR NOW WRAPS: on those same narrower windows the buttons at the top-right (Password, Portfolio…) were being clipped off the screen edge; the bar now wraps onto a second line instead, so every button is always reachable. Nothing else changed — if the app looks the same to you, these three were never biting on your screen size. ALSO RECENT — THREE READY-MADE RULE SETS, one tap each. 📐 My trading rules now opens with a Quick-start row: 🛡 DEFENDER — banks wins early and hates drawdowns (🎯12 🛑6 📦8% 🔢6 · 🪤 trail 6, 🛡 break-even +4, ⏱ 8 days · every don't-chase gate on with a strict 5% day-jump limit). ⚖ ALL-ROUNDER — the starter kit the Lessons teach (🎯20 🛑8 📦10% 🔢8 · 🪤8, 🛡+5, ⏱10 · gates on, day-jump 8): wide enough that normal daily wobble doesn't shake you out, tight enough that a real reversal gets caught. ⚡ HUNTER — fewer, bigger, longer (🎯30 🛑10 📦15% 🔢5 · 🪤10, 🛡+6, ⏱15 · green-day gate on, RSI gate OFF on purpose — momentum traders deliberately buy strength). A tap only FILLS the form — nothing changes until you press 💾 Save my rules, so you can look before you leap. And a sensible new default: BRAND-NEW installs now start on the full All-rounder (including the trailing/break-even/time exits and the don't-chase gates), so the app's out-of-the-box behaviour matches what the Lessons recommend. If you've EVER saved rules, nothing changes — your exact settings are kept, and older saved records keep their 0 = off values for the newer settings they never used. The 📐 My Rules lesson gained a step covering all three sets — start on All-rounder and let your Scoreboard, not your mood, argue you into another. ALSO RECENT — THE TRAINING OVERHAUL: the 🎓 Lessons course has been rebuilt — 27 lessons (up from 22) and 91 quick-quiz questions (up from 47), with practice trading and the Auto-pilot given the full-detail treatment. 📈 PRACTICE TRADING is now a complete 13-step course: market vs limit orders (and why your fill is honestly the NEXT day's open), position sizing and the $500 ASX minimum, why a $500 parcel starts about 4% behind on brokerage, setting your 🎯 and 🛑 BEFORE you buy, the trade-note habit, unrealised vs realised P/L, the benchmark line ('am I beating do-nothing?'), partial sells and the ↩ undo, reading your stats bar — why win rate alone lies and expectancy is the one number that must be positive — and the graduation rule. 🆕 FIVE NEW LESSONS: 📐 My trading rules, every rule explained with worked examples (including the 🪤 trailing stop, 🛡 break-even and ⏱ time stop); 🤖 Auto-pilot I — what it is, what it can NEVER do (practice money only, no real-broker path exists), and every safety rail in one place; Auto-pilot II — 🎯 My Picks and the 🔁 repeat cycle, and why a stop-out PAUSES the repeat instead of churning; Auto-pilot III — the exits, with a dollar-by-dollar trailing-stop walk-through and the gap-honesty rule (why a crash fills at the open, not your stop price); Auto-pilot IV — the don't-chase gates (green-day-only, skip RSI ≥ 70, skip day-jump > X%) and the Scoreboard, with expectancy explained in one breath and the 20-trade graduation test. EVERY LESSON now ends in a 📝 Quick quiz — 91 questions in all, wrong guesses cost nothing, and every answer explains WHY. Existing lessons deepened too: the grouped sidebar, 🖥 full screen on report windows, the 🕵️ 'Who's actually buying?' ownership callout, the report card's honest fine print, and two new entries in Common Mistakes — chasing big movers, and betting the farm on one share. In training mode, hover-help now covers every Auto-pilot and exits control. One heads-up: quiz progress is now remembered by lesson NAME instead of position (so future course growth never scrambles it) — your ✓ passed ticks reset once with this update, and retaking on the new content is intended. Find it all under 🎓 Lessons. ALSO RECENT — SMARTER EXITS + A SCOREBOARD (the day-trader batch; all practice money, all optional). 🪤 TRAILING STOP: set a trail % in 📋 My trading rules and your stop RATCHETS — always X% below the highest close since you bought, only ever moving up. A position that runs +15% can no longer round-trip back to your old −8% stop; the app protects the win. Honest by construction: each day's stop is built only from PRIOR days' closes (a real trailing order updates after the close), and every ratchet step is logged on the holding. 🛡 BREAK-EVEN MOVE: once a close is +X% above your entry, the stop jumps to your buy price — the 'free trade'. ⏱ TIME STOP: positions that haven't resolved in N data days exit at the next open, freeing the cash — dead money goes back to work. DON'T-CHASE GATES for Auto-pilot's scanner: 🟢 green-day-only (your own 'wait for a green day' rule, now enforced by the machine), skip RSI ≥ 70, skip day-jumps bigger than X%. And 📊 THE SCOREBOARD: a button in the Auto-pilot panel that judges the bot on ITS OWN closed trades — win rate, average win vs loss, expectancy per trade, profit factor, max drawdown, brokerage paid, and how each exit fired (🎯/🪤/🛑/⏱). Positive expectancy after costs, over enough trades, is the only number that should ever graduate these rules beyond practice. Notes: trail and time exits do NOT pause a 🔁 repeat pick (a trailed exit is usually a protected win) — only a true 🛑 stop does; trail exits get sell slippage like stops; exit rules manage every practice holding while My Rules is ON (0 = off). ALSO RECENT — 🖥 FULL SCREEN ON REPORT WINDOWS. Every window that pops up — reports, 🔬 Deep Dive, your portfolio, the report card, the briefing — now has a '🖥 Full screen' button next to ⛶ Max. One tap fills your ENTIRE monitor with that window (edge to edge, browser full screen and all); press Esc, tap it again, or simply Close, and everything returns exactly to its previous size. ⛶ Max keeps its familiar large-in-window behaviour for when you don't want to leave the page. ALSO RECENT — 🔁 REPEAT PICKS. Tick the new 🔁 box on a 🎯 pick and it CYCLES: practice limit buy at your price → sells at your 🎯 → buys again at your buy point, over and over, with a cycle counter in the pick list and diary ('🔁 cycle 3'). The important safety rule: if a cycle ends on your 🛑 stop — a loss — the repeat PAUSES ('⏸ stopped out — repeat paused') instead of buying straight back into a falling share; re-add the pick deliberately if you want another go. While a cycle's position is open, the pick simply waits — it can never double-buy. Still practice money only. ALSO RECENT — 💵 CHOOSE YOUR AMOUNTS. Auto-pilot now takes the dollar amount you want to invest: a global '$ per buy' for the scanner, and an 'Invest $' on each 🎯 pick. First one set wins — pick $ first, then the global $, and anything left blank falls back to your 📦 %-of-account rule exactly as before. Your 📦 rule stays as a GUARDRAIL: if an amount you set would breach it, the buy is trimmed to the cap and the decision diary says 'trimmed by 📦' so nothing happens silently. The $500 ASX minimum parcel applies to every amount, and it's all still practice money only. ALSO RECENT — 🤖 AUTO-PILOT LEARNS YOUR PRICES. Two additions, still practice-only and OFF unless you switched it on. 🎯 MY PICKS: name a share and YOUR prices — a buy point, and optionally your own sell point and stop. Auto-pilot places a practice LIMIT order at your buy price (sized by your 📦 rule, brokerage charged), and when it fills, your sell point becomes the 🎯 and your stop the 🛑 — your prices override the % rules, which stay as the fallback for anything you leave blank. A pick DISARMS ITSELF once it fills, so it can never re-buy in a loop; add it again if you want another go. Adding a pick for a share you ALREADY hold applies your sell/stop to that holding instead. SIGNAL GATES: tick which signals the scanner must be showing before it buys — 🚀 Breakout, 🕵️ Informed?, 🏦 Inst?, ⤴ MA cross, 🧑‍💼 Director ✓, 🥇 Vol record (at least one ticked must show; none ticked = off, exactly as before). Every decision still lands in the diary with its reason, and your 🔔 briefing reports any pick it placed. ALSO RECENT — 🤖 AUTO-PILOT (PRACTICE) — the app can now make practice buys BY YOUR RULES. It's OFF until you switch it on inside 📋 My trading rules, and it only ever uses practice money — it cannot touch a real broker or real dollars. Once per fresh data day it applies the entry rules YOU set (buy from 🎯 Smart Money or the 🏆 Top 20, optional minimum Watch Score, optional 'quiet only — no news'), and queues practice buys through exactly the same realistic path as your own taps: the order fills at the NEXT day's open, brokerage is charged, and your 🎯 take-profit and 🛑 stop-loss are set from your rules. It respects your 📦 max-per-share, 🔢 max positions and the 💧 liquidity floor, skips any parcel under $500 (the ASX minimum), and keeps a DECISION DIARY — every buy and every skip, with the reason in plain words. Your 🔔 briefing tells you whatever it queued. Why practice-only? So you can prove your rule-set works over weeks, with zero risk, before any real money ever follows it — discipline first, always. A ▶ Run-now button lets you try it instantly. ALSO RECENT — A CLEARER SIDEBAR: your Instant Reports are now organised instead of one long list. A gold ⭐ START YOUR DAY row sits at the top — 📊 Market Today, 🏆 Top 20 to Watch and 🎯 Smart Money, the three-tap daily routine the Lessons teach. Below it, the rest are grouped by what they're for: 🔎 TODAY'S ACTION (Sharp Mover, Unusual Activity, Volume Surge, Gap Report — what stood out this session), 🌱 BUILDING QUIETLY (Quiet Movers, Pullback in Uptrend, Strong Trends, Recovery Watch — patient setups), and 🧩 COMPOSITE (All Signals). Tap any group's heading to fold it away — the app remembers your choice on each device — and your 📋 Signal report card, 🔔 briefing and 📋 My trading rules now sit higher up, easier to reach. Every report is still here and works exactly as before; nothing was removed. One small change: drag-to-reorder of the report buttons is paused while the grouped layout beds in — tell us if you miss it. FASTER — three under-the-hood speed-ups: the search box now waits for you to pause typing before it filters (no more re-drawing the whole table on every keystroke — noticeably smoother on tablets and phones), the Market Radar tile counts are computed in a single pass over your shares instead of fifteen, and sorting the table does its maths once per share instead of over and over. Same numbers, same behaviour, less work. STRONGER PASSWORDS — the app's password protection has been upgraded to current best-practice strength (600,000 hashing rounds, up from 150,000). Nothing to do on your end: new and changed passwords use the new strength immediately, and your existing password is upgraded silently the next time you log in — it takes a fraction of a second and you won't notice it. SAFER DEFAULT — brand-new installs now start with the 💧 Liquidity floor set to ≥$50k traded per day, so every report skips shares that are hard to buy and sell at a fair price. The gold 💧 badge shows it's on; one tap (Type tab → Any) turns it off, and your choice is remembered on that device from now on. If you're already using the app, nothing changes — your existing setting is kept exactly as it is. FIX — the 🕵️ 'Who's actually buying?' links now land on pages that really show the data: the old shareholder-registry page some links pointed to has been retired by its publisher, so 👥 'Top shareholders & insiders' now opens a targeted search that reliably surfaces the full ownership picture (top holders with percentages, plus director buys and sells) for any share; 🧑‍💼 is relabelled 'Director filings (3Y)' and opens the official ASX filings page; and the fine print now tells you exactly what to look for — director buys/sells are the announcements titled 'Change of Director's Interest Notice', a new 5%+ holder lodges a 'substantial holder' notice, and the complete top-20 register is published in each company's annual report. (Tip: practice-mode shares that aren't real ASX listings won't match live pages — try a real code.) NEW — 🕵️ 'WHO'S ACTUALLY BUYING?': open the 🔬 Deep Dive (or 🖨 printable report) on a share showing a quiet-accumulation footprint and a new gold panel now links you straight to the public ASX filings that reveal whether a real buyer is behind the move — 👥 substantial holders (5%+), 🧑‍💼 director trades (Appendix 3Y), and 📢 announcements, one tap each. The footprint stays labelled as our estimate from price and volume; the filings are fact. Note: holdings under 5% aren't publicly disclosed, so quiet buying below that level won't show. It's the fastest way to check whether an 'informed' footprint has a real name behind it. FIX — the 🔔 'What's changed for me' briefing no longer opens twice: if you opened the app on yesterday's saved data and it then quietly refreshed to today's, the briefing used to pop once for the old data and again for the new. It now opens just once when fresh data lands — a genuine new trading day still gets its own briefing, and the 🔔 button reopens the latest any time. NEW — 📋 MY TRADING RULES NOW HAS AN ON/OFF SWITCH: some traders won't want the rules feature at all, so the 📋 My trading rules page now opens with a clear ON/OFF toggle at the top. Turn it OFF and the feature goes fully dormant — practice buys stop auto-filling your 🎯 target and 🛑 stop, and the 🔔 briefing stops flagging any rule breaches — while every number you saved is kept for whenever you switch it back on. Default is ON, so nothing changes if you're already using it; if you never wanted it, one tap makes it disappear from your day. FIX — searching a ticker no longer wipes the report you're viewing: while a report or scan is on screen, the search box now NARROWS that report in place (the same way the watchlist search works) instead of clearing it and searching the whole market. To search every share again, clear the report (the ✕ on its banner, or Clear-all). On the normal table, search is unchanged. NEW — ⛶ FULL SCREEN: a button in the top bar expands the app to fill your whole screen; press Esc (or tap the button again) to come back. FIX — in light mode, the loaded-market status pill (‘ASX · date · N stocks · live’) was staying dark; it now switches to a light style like the rest of the app. FIX — the 🔔 'What's changed for me' briefing could reopen by itself every few seconds after you closed it; it now opens at most once per fresh data day and stays closed when you dismiss it (tap the 🔔 button any time to reopen it yourself). NEW — ☀️ LIGHT & DARK MODE: a sun/moon button in the top bar switches the whole app between the dark theme and a new light theme — easier on the eyes in daylight or if you simply prefer a bright screen. It remembers your choice on each device, and the very first time you open it, it quietly matches whatever light-or-dark setting your phone or computer already uses. The accents (gold, green, red) keep their identity, just deepened a touch so everything stays crisp and readable on white. NEW — 📋 MY TRADING RULES: a place to write your own plan and have the app help you keep it. Set your take-profit %, stop-loss %, the most you'll put in any one share, and how many positions you'll hold at once — plus a free-text checklist of your own rules ('wait for a green day', 'never average down', 'review every Sunday'). Your 🎯 target and 🛑 stop then auto-fill every practice buy, and if you go over your max size, hold more positions than your rule allows, or drift past your stop, your 🔔 briefing flags it gently — never a block. It's your plan, in your words: a discipline aid, clearly not advice. SECURITY — a routine re-audit of everything drawn on screen (after this batch of new reports and the briefing) found one spot to tighten: confirmed-announcement headlines were cleaned of any hidden markup when freshly fetched, but not when re-read from their saved cache. That's now cleaned the moment it enters the app, everywhere, no matter the source — a purely protective change with no effect on normal use. FIX — a leftover ticker search no longer follows you around: searching a share and then opening ⭐ My Watchlist used to silently filter the list (e.g. '1 in My Watchlist' when 27 are starred). Every way into the watchlist — the ⭐ button, the ⚙ Lists panel, switching lists, or tapping a list in the 🔔 briefing — now clears the search box first — the full list always shows. Running any report already cleared it. NEW REPORT — 🕳 GAP REPORT: shares that OPENED 3%+ above or below yesterday's close (overnight news or a rush of morning orders), split into gapped & HELD (buyers stayed all day — strength) vs gapped & FADED (the jump fizzled — a common trap), with a tap-through summary panel grouping every gap by what happened next. It uses the daily opens the app now stores (part of the candles fix) — if most of your shares don't have opens saved yet, one 🔄 Refresh/redownload fills them in and the report says so honestly. NEW REPORT — 🧱 STRONG TRENDS: the strongest up-trend structure there is — price above the 20-day average, above the 50-day, above the 200-day (the full 'stack') — ranked by trend quality: steady slope, not overstretched, quiet accumulation. If your shares don't have 200 days of stored history yet, it tells you exactly that instead of just showing nothing. STICKY LIQUIDITY FLOOR — your 💧 Liquidity filter (Type tab) now SURVIVES every scan instead of being cleared: set '≥$250k/day' once and Smart Money, Top 20, Gap Report and every other scan quietly skip the un-tradeable shares (the 💧 badge stays visible so you always know it's on; Clear all still resets it). CLEARER BUTTONS — 🚨 Sharp Mover now says what it really is (the SIZE of today's move vs that share's own normal day) and 🔍 Unusual Activity too (five suspicious PATTERNS in HOW it traded) — two different questions, now impossible to confuse. TIDIER — the More-scans overlay retired the 📈 Momentum and ⚡ Volume Spike presets (the 🎯 Smart Money and 🔥 Volume Surge buttons do the same job better) and the remaining presets are relabelled with a plain-English explainer. 🔔 BRIEFING — What's changed for me shows your watchlists by NAME: holdings first, then each list with how many flagged today ('2 of 8 flagged'), an explicit 'all quiet today' when none did, and tap a list's name to open it. It checks YOUR practice holdings and watchlists each time fresh data loads — targets and stops hit or near, big moves, confirmed price-sensitive news, volume surges and trend crosses — and opens by itself once per new data day, only when there's something to say. CANDLES FIXED — candlestick view finally works: the daily refresh had been quietly wiping the open/high/low that candles need. Stored history is now preserved and grows daily; 🔄 Refresh/redownload restores full-depth candles immediately, and until then the chart draws clearly-labelled approximate candles instead of refusing. Candles work in demo mode too. FIX — the portfolio's 'return % is not trustworthy' warning behaves: tiny rounding residue no longer warns, bigger differences show the exact $ amount with a one-tap '✓ This balance is right — stop warning' that sticks, and it only returns if something NEW drifts. REPORT CARD — every flag's fires split into 📢 news-driven vs 🔇 quiet, plus how many WINNERS had news behind them; fires the app's announcement log can't cover are marked 'unknown', never guessed. CHARTS — professional refresh: round-number gridlines on the right axis, month ticks, dotted last-price line, soft glow, live price readout in the header. FILTERS — 💧 Liquidity ($ traded/day: Any / ≥$50k / ≥$250k / ≥$1M) replaces the old market-cap filter, and an RSI zone filter (Oversold ≤30 / Neutral / Overbought ≥70). 🪜 PULLBACK IN UPTREND report: uptrend shares that pulled back to the 20-day line with RSI cooled to 35–55. The 🎓 Lessons and 📖 Help cover all of this, including the two new reports. PAPER TRADING — the ↩ undo button removed from trade history (closed trades stay honest). DEMO — practice shares carry their correct real-world names, and demo data can't fake a gap. All figures are end-of-day estimates, not advice.
